task: Build a new pi extension at extensions/AskUserQuestion/ that registers a tool named exactly AskUserQuestion, with FULL parity to Claude Code AskUserQuestion. Schema: { questions: [{ header: string, question: string, multiSelect: boolean, options: [{ label: string, description: string }] }] }. Behavior: in interactive mode (ctx.hasUI true) render an interactive dialog via ctx.ui.custom that lets the user move with arrow keys, select one option (or toggle multiple when multiSelect), optionally type a free-text note, and confirm; iterate through all questions in sequence; return a structured result mapping each question header to the chosen option label(s) plus any note. In headless mode (ctx.hasUI false) do NOT hang: return a structured result indicating UI is unavailable. Also write a unit test file extensions/AskUserQuestion/test/logic_test.sh (or .ts) that tests the pure selection logic (single vs multi toggle, notes) and the headless fallback shape, runnable without a live terminal. Keep the selection logic as a pure exported function so it is unit-testable. Cite pi UI primitives you rely on (ctx.ui.custom, Container/Text/Input from pi-tui).

summary: Added the AskUserQuestion pi extension with interactive sequential question dialogs, pure testable selection logic, and a structured headless fallback.
The implementation looks genuine. Let me verify the pi-tui primitives and ctx.ui.custom API actually exist as used.

feedback: Founder tested the live dialog. Three changes needed: (1) NAVIGATION: allow moving between questions forward AND back (step back to previous question to revise an earlier answer before final submit); show ALL question headers in the dialog so the user sees the full set, with the current one indicated. (2) PER-CHOICE NOTES: for each SELECTED option, allow attaching an optional free-text note shown directly under that option (works for both single- and multi-select; in multi-select each selected option can have its own note). (3) KEEP the existing whole-question note section as well — so there is a per-choice note under each selected option AND one overall note field for the whole question. Update the schema/return shape so the result captures per-choice notes (keyed by option label) plus the whole-question note, and update logic_test.sh to cover per-choice note set/clear, the whole-question note, and back/forward navigation state. Keep selection+note logic as pure exported functions.

summary: Implemented the AskUserQuestion pi extension with interactive UI, headless fallback, pure selection logic, and tests.
Let me verify the extension conventions match the repo and that imports are valid.
Added back/forward question navigation, visible full question header list, per-choice notes keyed by selected option label, and preserved whole-question notes.
I'll inspect the extension directory and files.

feedback: Founder tested round 2 live (per-choice + whole-question notes and back/forward nav all work). Now SIMPLIFY and restyle the dialog layout/keys: (1) LAYOUT: render the question headers as a HORIZONTAL ROW OF TABS at the top, each a small box like [1. Database] [2. Features], with the current question tab visually highlighted/active; below the tabs, show ONLY the current question prompt and its options listed vertically. (2) KEYBINDINGS (make these exact and show a one-line hint at the bottom): Left/Right arrows switch between question tabs; Up/Down arrows move the highlighted option within the current question; Space selects (single) or toggles (multi) the highlighted option; Tab cycles focus into the note fields — first the per-choice note for the currently highlighted option, then the whole-question note — and Tab again returns focus to the option list; Enter submits ALL questions and closes the dialog. Escape in a note field returns to the option list without losing other input. (3) Keep BOTH note types (per-choice note keyed by option label + one whole-question note) and the existing return shape. Update logic_test.sh if navigation/focus state changes. Keep selection/note/navigation logic as pure exported functions. Do not regress headless fallback.

summary: Restyled AskUserQuestion with horizontal tabs and exact requested keybindings, while preserving per-choice/whole-question notes and headless fallback. Added pure exported focus-cycle logic and tests.
Let me verify the extension structure matches the harness conventions and imports are valid.
